claimText: India is partnering with French aerospace major Safran to manufacture advanced jet engines for the 5th generation Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA).
evidenceExcerpt: India is partnering with French aerospace major Safran to manufacture advanced jet engines for the AMCA programme.
examUse: Statement-based question on international defence partnerships and technology transfers.
distractorHooks: Partnering with US-based General Electric (GE) instead of Safran
Manufacturing engines for 4th generation aircraft instead of 5th generation

claimText: The Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) programme is led by the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) under the DRDO.
evidenceExcerpt: The AMCA programme is led by the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) under DRDO.
examUse: Institution-feature question identifying the nodal agency for indigenous defence projects.
distractorHooks: Led exclusively by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL)
Led by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)

claimText: Currently, only the United States, Russia, and China possess operational 5th generation fighter aircraft.
evidenceExcerpt: Only the US (F-22, F-35), Russia (Su-57), and China (J-20) currently have operational 5th generation aircraft.
examUse: Statement-based question on global military capabilities and operational 5th generation fighters.
distractorHooks: Including India or France in the list of countries with operational 5th generation aircraft
Stating that only the US and Russia have operational 5th generation aircraft

claimText: 5th generation fighter aircraft are characterized by features such as stealth capabilities, twin engines, supercruise, and internal weapons bays.
evidenceExcerpt: 5th generation fighters feature stealth capabilities, twin engines, supercruise, and internal weapons bays.
examUse: Feature-based question identifying the defining characteristics of 5th generation fighter jets.
distractorHooks: External weapons bays instead of internal
Single engine as a defining feature of 5th generation

claimText: 4.5th generation fighter jets, such as the Rafale and Eurofighter Typhoon, incorporate stealth and radar absorbent materials to enhance 4th generation capabilities.
evidenceExcerpt: 4.5th generation fighters (e.g., Rafale, Eurofighter Typhoon) added stealth and radar absorbent materials to extend 4th generation capabilities.
examUse: Correct-pair or comparison question distinguishing between 4.5th and 5th generation fighter jets.
distractorHooks: Classifying the Rafale as a 5th generation fighter
Stating that 4.5th generation fighters lack radar absorbent materials
Classifying the MiG-29 or F-16 as 4.5th generation fighters
